response.justification: Strong geospatial domain background (GISCollective, Green Map) and some high-throughput distributed-systems exposure (Sociomantic) align well with the routing-pipeline context, but the core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is essentially absent. The only C/C++ work is a brief National Instruments stint ~2000-2001 and a bachelor thesis; no Rust. Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js with DLang as the systems language. Worth a screening call only if the team is open to a candidate who would need to ramp on modern C++/Rust, with the geospatial expertise as the offsetting bet. Resume also contains date inconsistencies that warrant clarification.
strengths: Deep geospatial / GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
High-throughput, low-latency systems work at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching)
Polyglot systems mindset (DLang, C/C++, Java) and stated comfort moving across the stack
concerns: No modern C++ experience; C/C++ work appears limited to early-career and a thesis project
No routing / navigation tile or map-data pipeline experience; resume also has overlapping/contradictory dates
